gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/283)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/283)
text: إِذَا رُهِنْتَ عَبْداً أَوْ دَابَّةً فَمَاتَ فَلَا شَيْ‏ءَ عَلَيْكَ وَ إِنْ هَلَكَتِ الدَّابَّةُ أَوْ أَبَقَ الْغُلَامُ فَأَنْتَ ضَامِنٌ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ja’far Al Razzaz, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Hameed, from Sayf Bin Ameyra, from Mansour Bin Hazim, from Suleyman Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When you hold a mortgaged slave or an animal and he dies, so there is nothing upon you, but if the animal is damaged or the slave absconds, so you are responsible’.
en.sarwar: 18. Muhammad ibn Ja’far al-Razzaz has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Hamid from Sayf ibn ‘Amirah from Mansur ibn Hazim from Sulayman ibn Khalid who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If you hold a slave or stumper as al-Rahn (security deposit) and they die; there is nothing upon you, but if the stumper is destroyed or the slave runs away you are responsible for it.’”
